goals: Deliver seven color grading effect nodes using the established GLSL + ModernGL + WebGL2 preview pattern.
Each node integrates seamlessly with `CoolVideoGenerator` for frame-by-frame video rendering.
Neutral/default parameters must produce no perceptible change to the source image (safe defaults).

userStories.title: Brightness / Contrast Effect Node
userStories.description: As a ComfyUI artist, I want a node that adjusts image brightness and contrast so that I can lighten, darken, or increase/decrease tonal range in my workflow.

acceptanceCriteria.text: Node `CoolBrightnessContrastEffect` appears in ComfyUI node browser.
Live WebGL2 preview widget reflects parameter changes in real time.
Default params (0, 0) produce no visible change to the source image.

userStories.title: HSL (Hue / Saturation / Lightness) Effect Node
userStories.description: As a ComfyUI artist, I want a node that shifts hue, saturation, and lightness so that I can remap colors or desaturate/saturate images.

acceptanceCriteria.text: Node `CoolHSLEffect` appears in ComfyUI node browser.
Inputs: `hue_shift` FLOAT [-180.0, 180.0, default 0.0], `saturation` FLOAT [-1.0, 1.0, default 0.0], `lightness` FLOAT [-1.0, 1.0, default 0.0].
Live WebGL2 preview widget reflects parameter changes in real time.
Default params (0, 0, 0) produce no visible change to the source image.

userStories.title: Color Temperature Effect Node
userStories.description: As a ComfyUI artist, I want a node that shifts the color temperature of an image warmer or cooler so that I can correct white balance or set a mood.

acceptanceCriteria.text: Node `CoolColorTemperatureEffect` appears in ComfyUI node browser.
Inputs: `temperature` FLOAT [-1.0, 1.0, default 0.0] (negative = cool/blue, positive = warm/orange), `tint` FLOAT [-1.0, 1.0, default 0.0] (greenâ€“magenta axis).
Live WebGL2 preview widget reflects parameter changes in real time.
Default params (0, 0) produce no visible change to the source image.

userStories.title: Curves (RGB Lift / Gamma / Gain) Effect Node
userStories.description: As a ComfyUI artist, I want lift/gamma/gain controls per channel so that I can perform precise tonal adjustments to shadows, midtones, and highlights independently.

acceptanceCriteria.text: Node `CoolCurvesEffect` appears in ComfyUI node browser.
Inputs: `lift` FLOAT [0.0, 1.0, default 0.0], `gamma` FLOAT [0.1, 4.0, default 1.0], `gain` FLOAT [0.0, 4.0, default 1.0] (applied uniformly across RGB).
Live WebGL2 preview widget reflects parameter changes in real time.
Default params (lift=0, gamma=1, gain=1) produce no visible change to the source image.

userStories.title: Color Balance Effect Node
userStories.description: As a ComfyUI artist, I want separate tint controls for shadows, midtones, and highlights so that I can apply split-toning and cinematic color looks.

acceptanceCriteria.text: Node `CoolColorBalanceEffect` appears in ComfyUI node browser.
Live WebGL2 preview widget reflects parameter changes in real time.
All defaults at 0.0 produce no visible change to the source image.

userStories.title: Sepia / Black & White / Duotone Effect Node
userStories.description: As a ComfyUI artist, I want a node that converts an image to sepia tone, black & white, or a duotone look so that I can apply classic and stylized monochrome aesthetics.

acceptanceCriteria.text: Node `CoolToneMappingEffect` appears in ComfyUI node browser.

Inputs: `mode` COMBO ["none", "bw", "sepia", "duotone"], `intensity` FLOAT [0.0, 1.0, default 1.0], `shadow_r/g/b` FLOAT [0.0, 1.0, default 0.0/0.0/0.0], `highlight_r/g/b` FLOAT [0.0, 1.0, default 1.0/1.0/1.0].
Live WebGL2 preview widget reflects parameter changes in real time.
Mode "none" produces no visible change to the source image.

userStories.title: LUT Application Effect Node
userStories.description: As a ComfyUI artist, I want a node that applies a .cube LUT file to an image so that I can use professional color grades in my ComfyUI workflow.

acceptanceCriteria.text: Node `CoolLUTEffect` appears in ComfyUI node browser.
Inputs: `lut_path` STRING (absolute or relative path to a `.cube` file), `intensity` FLOAT [0.0, 1.0, default 1.0].
Backend parses the `.cube` file and uploads LUT data as a 3D texture uniform (or flattened 2D strip) to the GLSL shader.
Live WebGL2 preview widget applies the LUT in real time (LUT data fetched via HTTP endpoint or embedded in EFFECT_PARAMS).
`intensity = 0.0` produces no visible change (original image); `intensity = 1.0` applies the full LUT.

functionalRequirements.description: Each effect node outputs `EFFECT_PARAMS` via `build_effect_params()` from `nodes/effect_params.py`.

Each effect has a dedicated GLSL fragment shader in `shaders/glsl/` (e.g. `brightness_contrast.frag`, `hsl.frag`, `color_temperature.frag`, `curves.frag`, `color_balance.frag`, `tone_mapping.frag`, `lut.frag`).
All shaders accept the required uniform contract: `uniform sampler2D u_image`, `uniform float u_time`, `uniform vec2 u_resolution`.
Each node has a dedicated Python file in `nodes/` and a JS extension in `web/` using `mount_effect_node_widget` from `web/effect_node_widget.js`.
All seven nodes are registered in `NODE_CLASS_MAPPINGS` and `NODE_DISPLAY_NAME_MAPPINGS` in `__init__.py`.
GL resource cleanup follows the project standard: `try/finally` releasing vao, vbo, fbo, renderbuffer, texture, program, ctx in that order.
For `CoolLUTEffect`, the `.cube` file parser must handle both 17Âł and 33Âł LUT sizes; unsupported sizes raise `ValueError`.
